rows.label: negative
rows.right: He/she/it **is not (isn't / 's not) playing** ... You/we/they **are not (aren't / 're not) playing** ...

content.subtitle: Use
items: Fixed plans and arrangements: I**'m meeting** Clara for lunch on Friday.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Future plans that have not yet been arranged are called 'intentions'. We do not use the present continuous for intentions. We use *be going to* instead. ✗ I**'m becoming** an explorer when I grow up.
We do not use the present continuous for predictions. We use *will* or *be going to* instead. ✗ Do you think you**'re enjoying** your trip to Berlin next week?

content.subtitle: Use
items: Certain future facts: The new bridge **will** be two kilometres long.
Predictions based on opinion or belief: You**'ll** love the food in Mexico.
Offers and requests: I**'ll** carry those bags for you.
Spontaneous decisions made at the moment of speaking: It's cold in here — I**'ll** close the window.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: For offers in question form with *I* and *we*, use *Shall*. ✓ **Shall** I drive you to the airport?
We do not use *will* for arrangements. ✗ We**'ll** visit my grandma this weekend.

rows.label: statement
rows.right: He/she/it **is ('s) going to** travel ... You/we/they **are ('re) going to** travel ...

rows.label: negative
rows.right: He/she/it **is not (isn't / 's not) going to** travel ... You/we/they **are not (aren't / 're not) going to** travel ...

rows.label: question
rows.right: **Is** he/she/it **going to** travel ...? **Are** you/we/they **going to** travel ...?

content.subtitle: Use
items: Plans and intentions for the future: I**'m going to** study medicine when I finish school.
Predictions based on something we can observe now: Watch out — that vase **is going to** fall!
Certain future facts: The new airport **is going to** be the biggest in Europe.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Scheduled events (timetables): The ferry **departs** at seven in the evening.

sections.title: A: Present continuous (Shelley's diary)
sections.instructions: Look at Shelley's diary and use the prompts to write sentences. Use the correct form of the present continuous.

items.sentence: On Monday, she ......
items.answer: is meeting Alison at Friends' Café

items.sentence: On Tuesday, she ......
On Wednesday, she ......
items.answer: is catching the train to Brighton

items.sentence: On Thursday, she ......
items.answer: is spending the day with Charlie in Brighton

items.sentence: On Friday, she ......
items.answer: is catching the train home at 10 am

items.sentence: On Saturday, she ......
items.answer: is working in Dad's shop all morning

sections.title: B: will or shall
sections.instructions: Complete using will or shall and the verbs in the box. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: This year, more than a million tourists ...... our local area.
I'm sure we ...... your bag soon. Where did you last see it?
...... you ...... me some money until Saturday?
Everything on the menu looks delicious! Erm ... I ...... Chicken Kiev, please.
I ...... you to the bus station, if you like.
One day, people ...... on Mars in special buildings.
No, there ...... any problems with delivering your new furniture next week.
...... we ...... at six to help you get things ready for dinner?

sections.title: C: be going to
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of be going to and the verbs in brackets. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: When I grow up, I ...... (play) guitar in a rock group!
Rick and Mark ...... (start) going to the gym twice a week.
...... Lauren ...... (tell) her mum about what happened?
I ...... (look) on the Internet for information about snowboarding.
No, Nadine ...... (invite) everyone from class – just her close friends.
...... Harry ...... (be) ready on time or not?
Careful! You ...... (break) something with that ball! Go outside!
I ...... (lie down) for half an hour. Call me at six o'clock.

sections.title: D: Present simple
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct present simple form of the verbs in the box.
sections.passage: I'm so excited about my holiday! My plane (1) ...... the airport here at nine o'clock and we (2) ...... in Paris two hours later. We then (3) ...... the train from the airport to the city. We'll have a great week, and then we (4) ...... back on the 17th. I can't wait!

sections.title: E: Circle the correct word or phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: Oscar says he **is doing / will do** the washing-up after dinner.
I'm a bit scared because I **am seeing / will see** the dentist this afternoon.
What **are you going to do / do you do** this evening?
**Shall you tell / Will you tell** Rupert I'm sorry about yesterday?
My dad **will grow / is going to grow** a beard, but my mum doesn't like the idea.
I have to revise tonight because we **are having / will have** an exam tomorrow.
I **am remembering / will remember** this day for the rest of my life!
**Do you go / Are you going** to Australia next Christmas?
I'm sure you **are passing / will pass** your driving test. Don't worry.
If you want me to, I **will complain / am going to complain** to the manager about it.
'Have you made plans for the summer?' 'Yes. ...... to Spain.'
'We're moving house tomorrow.' 'Really? ...... you with the furniture.'
'Do you need this paintbrush?' 'Ah, yes. ...... it to me, please?'
'What do you want to be when you grow up, Stevie?' '...... a scientist. That's what I want to do, anyway.'
'John is a better player than Martin, isn't he?' 'Oh, yes. ...... the match tomorrow, I expect.'
'The weather has been terrible, hasn't it?' 'Yes, I think ...... again later.'
